title: Review: Lessons 1-3
description: Mix of phrasal verbs from Daily Routine, Home, and Movement

points.title: Review Challenge
points.description: Test yourself with 10 mixed questions from Lessons 1-3

questions.sentence: Please ______ ______ (lift from the floor) that piece of paper.
questions.feedback: Correct! 'Pick up' means to lift something from a surface.

questions.sentence: I ______ ______ (left my bed) at 6 am to catch the early train.
questions.feedback: 'Got up' means you left the bed. 'Woke up' just means you opened your eyes.

questions.sentence: The music is too loud. Can you turn it ______?
questions.feedback: Yes! 'Turn down' means to reduce volume.

questions.question: Which phrasal verb means 'to enter a car'?
questions.feedback: Correct! Use 'get in' for small vehicles like cars and taxis.
With pronouns, the object goes between the verb and particle: 'Pick them up'.

questions.context: A: I'm so tired. I've been working all day. B: Why don't you ______ ______ on the sofa?
questions.feedback: 'Lie down' is perfect when you need a rest.

questions.sentence: When you finish playing, please ______ ______ (put back) your toys.
questions.feedback: 'Put away' means to return something to its proper place.

questions.sentence: It's raining. Let's not go ______ today.
questions.feedback: 'Go out' means to leave the house. 'Let's not go out' = let's stay home.

questions.question: What's the opposite of 'turn on'?
questions.feedback: 'Turn off' is the opposite of 'turn on'. 'Turn up' and 'turn down' are for volume.
